nodes.label: Tests for MonitorDaemon — main daemon loop that ties everything together.
All daemon tests assume market is open unless explicitly testing the guard.
Create a minimal MonitorRule for testing.
Create a mock DB rule object (MonitorRuleDB) that crud.db_rule_to_schema can han
When rules appear for a new user, start_user is called.
When a user's rules are all disabled/deleted, stop_user is called.
When rules change for an existing user, sync_rules is called.
A market tick triggers price rule evaluation and fires action.
Ticks for other instruments don't trigger rules.
After a tick, prev_indicator_values should be advanced to match     indicator_va
_check_time_rules must pass IST (UTC+5:30), not UTC, to EvalContext.      Time t
When a rule fires, ActionExecutor.execute is called.
place_order actions are skipped when market is closed, fire_count unchanged.
cancel_rule actions still execute outside market hours.
When evaluation returns fired=False, no action.
When no manual token is set, _poll_rules loads from DB via _load_access_token.
A running user whose token expires gets stopped.
A market tick triggers trailing_stop rule evaluation.
When evaluate_rule returns trigger_config_update, daemon persists it.
When trigger_config_update is None, no DB write happens.
_load_access_token delegates to get_user_upstox_token (which handles TOTP intern
Returns None when get_user_upstox_token returns None (expired, no TOTP).
Manual set_access_token takes precedence over DB lookup.
After a rule fires, fire_count is incremented in memory immediately.      This p

When a Long Entry fires, Short-side rules are disabled via also_cancel_rules
Entry rules are sorted before SL/Trail rules in the same tick.      Even if Shor
When a rule's activate chain re-enables exit rules, fire_count is reset to 0.
